title: Aruvikkuzhi Waterfalls
tagline: Aruvikkuzhi Waterfalls — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: Aruvikuzhy Waterfalls (not to be confused with Aruvikkuzhy Falls in pathanamthitta, Kerala) is a waterfall in Kottayam district in the Kerala state of India.
overview.description: Aruvikuzhy Waterfalls (not to be confused with Aruvikkuzhy Falls in pathanamthitta, Kerala) is a waterfall in Kottayam district in the Kerala state of India. It is situated 2 km (1.2 mi) from Pallickathode and 7.5 km (4.7 mi) from Pampady. The waterfall measures about 30 ft in height and active only during monsoon. In summer the river almost dries up.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kooroppada
topPlaces.description: Kooroppada is a village in Kottayam district, Kerala, India, situated 16 kilometres east to Kottayam town. Kooroppada village was formed in 1953 by merging Kooroppada with a certain portion of Pampady and Anikkad villages.

topPlaces.name: Anicad Sree Bhagavathi Temple
topPlaces.description: Anicad Sree Bhagavathi Temple is a well-known Hindu temple of the goddess Bhadrakali located at Anicad, Kottayam, Kerala, India. The temple is located on top of a hill.

topPlaces.name: Anicad (Kottayam)
topPlaces.description: Anickadu (a.k.a. Anicad or Anickad) is a village in Kottayam district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Moozhayil Sankaranarayana Temple
topPlaces.description: Moozhayil Sankaranarayana Temple is a Hindu temple located at Anicad, in Kottayam, Kerala, India. This is one of the 7 Sankaranarayana temples in Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Pallickathodu
topPlaces.description: Pallickathodu, also spelled Pallikkathodu which includes Anickadu village, is a town in eastern Kottayam district, Kerala, India. It falls under the Kanjirappally assembly constituency.

topPlaces.name: Lakkattoor
topPlaces.description: Lakkattoor is a small village in Kooroppada panchayat, about 17 km east of Kottayam, Kerala located between Kottayam, Ettumanoor, Pala and Ponkunnam. Surrounded by small hills, valleys, streams and rubber trees.

topPlaces.name: Akalakunnam
topPlaces.description: Akalakunnam is a village in Kottayam district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Pampady
topPlaces.description: Pampady is a large town in the Kottayam district of Kerala, India. It is situated midway between the backwaters of western Kerala and the mountains of the Western Ghats.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Aruvikkuzhi Waterfalls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Aruvikkuzhi Waterfalls is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Aruvikkuzhi Waterfalls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (68 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (58 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Aruvikkuzhi Waterfalls?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Aruvikkuzhi Waterfalls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Aruvikkuzhi Waterfalls?
faq.a: Stays in Aruvikkuzhi Waterfalls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Aruvikkuzhi Waterfalls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kooroppada, Anicad Sree Bhagavathi Temple, Anicad (Kottayam), Moozhayil Sankaranarayana Temple, Pallickathodu.
